task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

content.content:
budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx
helios_architecture_overview.txt
helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt
hr_performance_reviews_2026.txt
meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt
meridian_contract_signed.pdf
office_lease_renewal.txt
project_helios_status.txt

team_roster.txt
vendor_nda_template.txt

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
PROJECT HEALTH BRIEFING — Project Helios
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-08
Overall Status: ON TRACK (with minor-to-moderate risks)

================================================================================
1. CURRENT PROGRESS
================================================================================

Overall Assessment: Project Helios is ON TRACK with minor risks. Core platform
components are advancing, though several Q2 deliverables remain incomplete and
have been rescheduled into early Q3.

Milestone Progress:
  - Data ingestion module:        85% complete
  - API gateway:                  100% complete (delivered)
  - Client dashboard:             60% complete
  - Integration testing:          40% complete

Q2 Deliverables Status:
  DONE:
    - Requirements documentation v1.0
    - System architecture design
    - API gateway implementation
    - Development environment setup
    - Data schema finalization

  IN PROGRESS:
    - Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
    - Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)

  OVERDUE / RESCHEDULED:
    - Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
    - UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
    - Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Sprint 14 Goals:
  - Complete dashboard module v1
  - Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
  - Begin UAT preparation

Sprint 14 Testing Snapshot (as of 2026-07-11, ongoing):
  - 10 of 15 test cases executed
  - Authentication suite: 4/4 PASS
  - Data pipeline batch ingestion: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL (open)
  - Dashboard suite: 1/4 complete
  - Open high-severity defects: HEL-D-014 (date normalization), HEL-D-015 (memory overflow on large files)

Next Milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

================================================================================
2. KEY RISKS
================================================================================

Active High-Priority Risks (from Risk Register, last reviewed 2026-07-07):

  R-01  Meridian source data quality issues
        Likelihood: High | Impact: High | Score: 9
        Status: Active — date format blocker confirmed in Sprint 14
        Owner: Amir Hassan
        Detail: Meridian source data mixes ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY formats.
                Affects ~40% of source data. Dev team awaiting clean sample
                dataset from Meridian (due 2026-07-15). Estimated delay: 2-3 days.

  R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo
        Likelihood: Medium | Impact: High | Score: 6
        Status: Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
        Owner: Raj Patel
        Detail: Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending.
                Must be resolved before the 2026-07-25 product demo.

  R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets
        Likelihood: Medium | Impact: High | Score: 6
        Status: Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
        Owner: Derek Okafor
        Detail: Meridian has raised concern about load times. Benchmarking
                required before demo. Target: <2 seconds for 100k records.

  R-04  UAT timeline slippage
        Likelihood: High | Impact: Medium | Score: 6
        Status: Active — 1-week extension already consumed
        Owner: Sarah Chen
        Detail: UAT now scheduled Aug 17-28. Further slippage of >2 weeks
                pushes launch into Q4 and triggers Section 7.1 penalty review.

  R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor
        Likelihood: Low | Impact: High | Score: 3
        Status: Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
        Owner: Sarah Chen

  R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays
        Likelihood: Medium | Impact: Medium | Score: 4
        Status: Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
        Owner: Nadia Petrov
        Detail: Meridian IT confirmed UAT environment access by 2026-07-19;
                infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12.

Monitored / Lower-Priority:
  R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests (3 informal requests logged)
  R-08  Cloud cost overrun
  R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)

================================================================================
3. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
================================================================================

Q3 2026 Priorities (from Q3 Roadmap Draft):

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)
   - Confirmed with Meridian: demo must include live data, not mock data
   - Draft demo agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)
   - Note: UAT period already extended by 1 week from original plan

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target Launch Date: 2026-10-01

Proposed Q3 Feature Additions (subject to change control):
  - Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
  - Email alerting for pipeline failures
  - Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

================================================================================
4. ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION
================================================================================

1. GeoSync License Renewal — Time-Critical
   License must be resolved by 2026-07-14 to avoid jeopardizing the July 25
   product demo. Legal review is in progress. Leadership may need to escalate
   with Legal/Procurement if the renewal is not confirmed by mid-next week.

2. Data Quality Blocker with Meridian
   Date format inconsistencies are blocking progress on the data ingestion
   module. Meridian has committed to providing a clean sample dataset by
   2026-07-15. If this is delayed, the July 18 ingestion target and July 25
   demo are both at risk. Leadership may need to engage Meridian sponsorship
   (James Whitfield) if the dataset slips.

3. Overdue Performance Benchmarking
   The performance benchmarking report was due 2026-07-05 and has not started
   (task HEL-247). Meridian has explicitly raised dashboard load-time concerns.
   Derek Okafor owns this work; capacity and prioritization should be confirmed
   before the demo.

4. Large-File Ingestion Defect (HEL-D-015)
   Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k records due to memory overflow.
   Severity: HIGH. This directly threatens the Q3 throughput target of 500k
   records/min and the demo's live-ingestion requirement. Root cause is under
   investigation by Amir Hassan.

5. Key Person Dependency
   No backup tech lead has been identified for Derek Okafor. Given his ownership
   of the GeoSync license action, performance benchmarking, and technical
   leadership for the demo, this represents a single point of failure that
   leadership should address (cross-training or backup designation).

6. Launch Penalty Exposure
   Contract Section 7.1 penalty review is triggered if UAT slips more than
   2 weeks beyond the current schedule, pushing launch past 2026-10-01 into Q4.
   One week of UAT extension has already been consumed. Leadership should
   monitor UAT readiness closely and be prepared to protect the remaining
   schedule buffer.

7. UAT Environment Access Dependency
   UAT environment provisioning is overdue (original due date 2026-07-01) and
   blocked on Meridian IT. Meridian has committed to access by 2026-07-19 and
   an infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12. Delayed access compresses UAT prep
   time ahead of the Aug 17 start.

================================================================================
SUMMARY
================================================================================

Project Helios remains on track for the 2026-10-01 launch, with the critical
path running through: (1) resolving the Meridian data format blocker,
(2) securing the GeoSync license, (3) completing dashboard and integration
work by July 25 for the Meridian product demo, and (4) protecting the UAT
window from further slippage. Immediate leadership support is most needed on
the license renewal escalation path and Meridian data-quality dependency
management. Capacity for performance benchmarking and key-person coverage for
the tech lead role also warrant attention this week.

— End of Briefing —

content.text:
The comprehensive project health briefing has been prepared and saved as `project_health_briefing.txt`. It covers:

1. **Current Progress** — Milestone completion rates, Q2 deliverables status (done/in-progress/overdue), Sprint 14 goals and testing results
2. **Key Risks** — All active risks from the register with scores, owners, and status details
3. **Upcoming Q3 Commitments** — Integration completion, Meridian product demo (July 25), UAT (Aug 17–28), performance hardening, and launch prep toward the Oct 1 target
4. **Issues Needing Leadership Attention** — GeoSync license urgency, Meridian data quality blocker, overdue benchmarking, large-file ingestion defect, key-person dependency, launch penalty exposure, and UAT environment access delays
